title: ADR-0021: wiki-api edge — caller-token pass-through (never re-mint, never a service credential)
verdict_summary: Draft ADR (pending PO doc-ratify) that records the wiki-api edge token contract implemented and locked by ENG-1488. It is a decision doc, not a requirements doc: it MANDATES no new FR/NFR ids of its own and its build-shaped MUSTs (forward caller token byte-for-byte, reuse pkg/auth RequireScope, fail-closed on IdentityVerifyEnabled=false, propagate typed errors) are each carried by existing token-pass-through stories. FR-A4/A5 (which the ADR only cites) are in covers-union. Named components (orvex-wiki-api, orvex-studio-lib/pkg/auth, server.go/clients.go, the DoD test) all exist and are covered. Zero build gaps.

components.name: orvex-wiki-api (resource-agnostic verb-grammar edge facade)
components.quote: `orvex-wiki-api` (the resource-agnostic verb-grammar facade, FR-A4/A5) sits in front of two upstreams — the wiki engine and knowledge — and forwards every caller request to both.

components.name: orvex-studio-lib/pkg/auth (RequireScope, Principal, MultiIssuerVerifier)
components.quote: `orvex-studio-lib/pkg/auth` — `RequireScope`, `Principal`, `MultiIssuerVerifier` (true-external identity boundary, faked only in tests per CS §5).

components.name: libVerifierAdapter (wraps *auth.MultiIssuerVerifier)
components.quote: `libVerifierAdapter` (wraps `*auth.MultiIssuerVerifier`) exists in wiki-api today but is unwired — it requires issuer/JWKS config that this repo does not yet provision (tracked as a follow-up, not a gap in this ADR's decision).
